title: Recover a project decision from core routing and archival rationale

items.text: Core decision routing block: keep the benchmark outcome policy always attached and route detailed rationale to archival notes.

locator.quote: route detailed rationale to archival notes

items.text: Archival decision record: scenario outcomes use win, tie, loss, not_tested, blocked, or non_goal only when scenario evidence supports them.

locator.quote: use win, tie, loss, not_tested, blocked, or non_goal only when scenario evidence supports them

items.text: Archival project decision: core blocks stay separate from archival note search and Qdrant-derived retrieval.

locator.quote: core blocks stay separate from archival note search

items.text: Wrong claim: Letta comparison can be scored as an ELF win because ELF has core blocks.

answer.content: Use the always-attached core routing block to find the benchmark outcome policy, then cite archival notes for the detailed decision. The archival decision says to use win, tie, loss, not_tested, blocked, or non_goal only when scenario evidence supports them. It also says core blocks stay separate from archival note search and Qdrant-derived retrieval, so no ELF-over-Letta claim follows from ELF having core blocks.

claims.text: The core routing block points detailed decision rationale to archival notes.
Scenario outcomes use win, tie, loss, not_tested, blocked, or non_goal only when evidence supports them.
Core blocks stay separate from archival note search and Qdrant-derived retrieval.

timeline.summary: A core block kept the outcome-policy routing pointer always attached.
Archival notes recorded the detailed outcome policy and core-search boundary.

prompt.content: What is the benchmark outcome policy, and does having ELF core blocks make Letta a measured loss?

must_include.text: The core routing block points detailed decision rationale to archival notes.
Scenario outcomes use win, tie, loss, not_tested, blocked, or non_goal only when evidence supports them.
Core blocks stay separate from archival note search and Qdrant-derived retrieval.

must_not_include: Letta comparison can be scored as an ELF win because ELF has core blocks

required_evidence.quote: route detailed rationale to archival notes
use win, tie, loss, not_tested, blocked, or non_goal only when scenario evidence supports them
core blocks stay separate from archival note search

evidence_grounding.criteria: Cites core routing and archival decision evidence.

trap_avoidance.criteria: Avoids an unsupported Letta win or loss claim.

workflow_helpfulness.criteria: Explains how core memory and archival decision evidence work together.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true

acceptable_phrases: The fixture does not provide that evidence.
